Review /Users/vics/dev/structupath/herdr-browser for logic errors, edge cases, state bugs, and error-propagation failures. Context: herdr-browser is a Herdr terminal-multiplexer plugin — a driveable browser pane. bin/renderer.mjs (~551 lines, Node>=20, ES module) is a TUI renderer that polls agent-browser (daemon-backed headless Chrome) for screenshots/URL/title/console output and renders into the terminal via Kitty graphics or ANSI half-blocks (via chafa), maps mouse clicks back to page coordinates, has an address bar, scroll, history nav. scripts/*.sh implement plugin actions (open.sh, close.sh, browse.sh, browse-pane.sh, pane.sh, lib.sh shared helpers). herdr-plugin.toml is the manifest. tests/*.mjs are node:test suites (57 tests, currently passing — run `npm test` to confirm). Read every file fully (use offset/limit reads). Hunt: off-by-one geometry math in click mapping, race conditions between poll loop and user input, partial/corrupt PNG handling, session-name derivation mismatches between bash and JS, process cleanup on signals, integer parsing of env vars, escape-sequence sanitization completeness. Report concrete findings as P0 (correctness bug users will hit), P1 (edge case), P2 (nit), each with file:line, a one-line explanation, and a suggested fix. Do NOT edit any files — report only.

Acceptance level: attested

content.thinking:
I've read the full renderer.mjs (551 lines). Now let me note initial observations, then read the shell scripts.

Key observations so far on renderer.mjs:

1. **`deriveSession`** (lines 18-30):
   - If `HERDR_BROWSER_SESSION` set → use it.
   - Else if `HERDR_PLUGIN_CONFIG_DIR` has `session` file → read first line, strip whitespace.
   - Else if `HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID` → `herdr-ws-${id}`.
   - Else fallback: `cksum` of cwd → `herdr-cwd-${sum}`.
   
   Must stay in lockstep with `session_name()` in lib.sh. Need to compare with lib.sh. Potential mismatch: JS reads first line with `.split('\n')[0]` then strips ALL whitespace (`replace(/\s/g, '')`). Bash might use something different. Also the cksum call: `printf '%s\n' "$HB_CWD" | cksum` — passing via env to avoid injection. `.stdout.toString().split(' ')[0]` — cksum output is like `1234567890 12` (checksum byte-count). OK. But note: `spawnSync(...).stdout` — if spawnSync fails (sh not found), stdout could be null → `.toString()` would throw. Edge case on non-unix. Node>=20, probably fine on macOS/Linux. P2.
   
   Also: JS `deriveSession` checks `HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID` before falling back to cksum of cwd. What does lib.sh do? Need to check ordering matches exactly.

2. **`reconcileConsole`** — logic for ring buffer reconciliation. Let me trace:
   - `prev.count === 0` → all new.
   - `entries.length < prev.count` → shrink → return all with marker. This handles external clear and also rotation below count? Hmm — if ring is saturated at 1000 and prev.count was 1000, entries.length stays 1000 (equal, not less). So shrink only on clear. OK.
   - `prev.count < ringSize && entries.length >= prev.count`: head-alignment check. head = first prev.count texts. aligned = tail of prev matches head window at the right offset. Then newEntries = entries.slice(prev.count). Looks right: if buffer grew from count N to M with no eviction, first N entries unchanged; we verify the tail of what we last saw matches positions N-tail.length..N. Then everything after N is new. Good.
   - Else (ring saturated or head mismatch): search for the latest occurrence of prev.tail (progressively shorter suffixes) in texts; newEntries = entries after that window end. If nothing found → all entries, marker.
   
   Subtle issue: when `prev.count < ringSize` is false (prev.count >= 1000) and entries.length == prev.count == 1000, the head check is skipped entirely (condition requires prev.count < ringSize). So it goes to the suffix search. That's the documented design. The suffix search with winLen from tail.length (8) down to 1. `win = prev.tail.slice(prev.tail.length - winLen)` — the last winLen entries of tail. #!/usr/bin/env bash
# Open action: navigate the workspace browser session to a URL, or attach
# view-only when invoked with no URL (action invoke and keybindings cannot
# pass arguments in herdr 0.7.x, so URL-less is the primary invoke path).
set -uo pipefail
cd "${HERDR_PLUGIN_ROOT:-$(dirname "$0")/..}" || exit 1
. scripts/lib.sh

require_agent_browser

url="${1:-${HERDR_PLUGIN_CLICKED_URL:-}}"
session="$(session_name)"

if [ -n "$url" ]; then
  if ! validate_url "$url"; then
    echo "herdr-browser: refusing URL (must start with http:// or https://): $url" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
  # If this call spawns the session daemon, let it self-reap after 30 min
  # idle instead of living forever (no-op for daemons agents already own).
  export AGENT_BROWSER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS="${AGENT_BROWSER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS:-1800000}"
  if ! agent-browser --session "$session" open "$url" >/dev/null; then
    echo "herdr-browser: agent-browser failed to open $url" >&2
    exit 3
  fi
fi

# Serialize concurrent opens: two invokes racing past the liveness check
# would each open a pane (herdr pane open is not idempotent). mkdir is the
# portable atomic lock; after ~5s of waiting the holder is presumed dead and
# the lock is stolen — a duplicate pane beats an open action that never acts.
lock="$(state_dir)/open-lock-${HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID:-default}"
tries=0
until mkdir "$lock" 2>/dev/null; do
  tries=$((tries + 1))
  if [ "$tries" -gt "${HERDR_BROWSER_LOCK_TRIES:-50}" ]; then
    rm -rf "$lock"
  fi
  sleep 0.1
done
trap 'rmdir "$lock" 2>/dev/null' EXIT

pidfile="$(pane_id_file)"
existing=""
[ -f "$pidfile" ] && existing="$(cat "$pidfile")"

if pane_alive "$existing"; then
  "$HERDR" plugin pane focus "$existing" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
else
  out="$("$HERDR" plugin pane open --plugin "$PLUGIN_ID" --entrypoint view \
    --placement split --direction right --focus)" || {
    echo "herdr-browser: failed to open pane" >&2
    exit 4
  }
  pane_id="$(parse_pane_id "$out")"
  if [ -n "$pane_id" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$pane_id" > "$pidfile"
  else
    echo "herdr-browser: warning: could not parse pane id from pane-open output" >&2
    rm -f "$pidfile"
  fi
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Pane entrypoint: run the renderer. Never exit instantly on a missing
# dependency — an exiting pane process closes the pane before the user can
# read the error.
cd "${HERDR_PLUGIN_ROOT:-$(dirname "$0")/..}" || exit 1

if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "herdr-browser: node not found on PATH (need Node.js >= 20)."
  echo "Install Node.js, then reopen this pane."
  sleep 600
  exit 1
fi

# Resolve the session once through the shared bash helper so the launcher and
# the renderer can never derive different names.
. scripts/lib.sh
HERDR_BROWSER_SESSION="$(session_name)"
export HERDR_BROWSER_SESSION

exec node bin/renderer.mjs

set -uo pipefail

  printf '%s\n' "$pane_id" >> "$(browse_ids_file)"

fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Interactive browser pane: carbonyl renders Chromium straight into the
# terminal with full mouse/keyboard support. Prompts for a URL when none was
# passed, so a bare keybinding acts as an address bar.
cd "${HERDR_PLUGIN_ROOT:-$(dirname "$0")/..}" || exit 1

if ! command -v carbonyl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "herdr-browser: carbonyl not found."
  echo "Install it with: npm install -g carbonyl"
  echo "Then reopen this pane."
  sleep 600
  exit 1
fi

url="${HERDR_BROWSE_URL:-}"
if [ -z "$url" ]; then
  printf 'URL: '
  IFS= read -r url
  if [ -z "$url" ]; then
    exit 0
  fi
  case "$url" in
    http://*|https://*) ;;
    *) url="https://$url" ;;
  esac
fi

zoom="${HERDR_BROWSE_ZOOM:-}"
if [ -z "$zoom" ] && [ -n "${HERDR_PLUGIN_CONFIG_DIR:-}" ] && [ -f "${HERDR_PLUGIN_CONFIG_DIR}/zoom" ]; then
  zoom="$(head -n1 "${HERDR_PLUGIN_CONFIG_DIR}/zoom" | tr -cd '0-9')"
fi

exec carbonyl --zoom="${zoom:-100}" "$url"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Close action: close this workspace's browser panes first (so the renderers
# exit cleanly), then end this plugin's browser session. Never touches other
# workspaces or other sessions.
set -uo pipefail
cd "${HERDR_PLUGIN_ROOT:-$(dirname "$0")/..}" || exit 1
. scripts/lib.sh

session="$(session_name)"
closed=0

close_pane() {
  [ -n "$1" ] || return 0
  if "$HERDR" pane close "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    closed=$((closed + 1))
  fi
  return 0
}

# Tracked panes: the view pane (open.sh) and any browse panes (browse.sh).
# Close unconditionally — a liveness pre-check that fails would strand a live
# pane, while closing an already-dead id is harmless.
for f in "$(pane_id_file)" "$(browse_ids_file)"; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r id; do close_pane "$id"; done < "$f"
    rm -f "$f"
  fi
done

# Safety net for untracked panes: list this workspace's panes and close any
# carrying this plugin's pane labels. Generic close, not `plugin pane close`:
# the plugin-scoped variant silently no-ops on panes from a previous plugin
# registration.
if [ -n "${HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID:-}" ] && command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  strays="$("$HERDR" pane list --workspace "$HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID" 2>/dev/null | node -e '
    let d = "";
    process.stdin.on("data", c => d += c).on("end", () => {
      let j; try { j = JSON.parse(d); } catch { return; }
      for (const p of (j.result && j.result.panes) || []) {
        if (p.pane_id && (p.label === "Browser" || p.label === "Browse")) {
          console.log(p.pane_id);
        }
      }
    });')"
  for stray in $strays; do close_pane "$stray"; done
fi

if command -v agent-browser >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  agent-browser --session "$session" close >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

sd="$(state_dir)"
ws="${HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID:-default}"
rm -f "$sd/shot-$ws.png" "$sd/shot-$ws.png.tmp"

# Surfaces in `herdr plugin log list` — close must never fail silently again.
echo "herdr-browser: closed $closed pane(s); session $session released"

Also: the fallback collision — ALL workspaces without HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID share the same session name `herdr-cwd-<cksum-of-plugin-root>`! Because PWD is always plugin root after cd. So two different workspaces (no workspace id env) → same agent-browser session → cross-workspace session sharing. Wait — is HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID always set by herdr? Probably yes in practice (herdr 0.7.x sets it for actions and panes). The fallback is for direct invocation. Hmm. But wait — actually, is the fallback cksum MEANT to be of the workspace cwd? The design "cwd hash" implies the user's project directory. Since every script cds to plugin root first, the hash is constant across all projects. Two different projects → same session → project A's pane shows project B's browser. That's a real semantic bug IF HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID is ever unset. Given herdr always sets it, P2. But also JS fallback: renderer run directly from arbitrary cwd → herdr-cwd-<cksum(cwd)> — DIFFERENT from what bash would compute for the same workspace (plugin root) → open.sh navigates session X; direct-run renderer watches session Y. P2 dev-only.
  
  Actually wait — more important: I need to check whether JS's `deriveSession` matches bash `session_name` in the CONFIG FILE case: bash `head -n1 file | tr -d '[:space:]'` vs JS `readFileSync.split('\n')[0].replace(/\s/g,'')`. Equivalent-ish. JS \s also strips unicode whitespace (nbsp) — bash [:space:] in C locale strips ASCII. Trivial.
  
  And env override: both use HERDR_BROWSER_SESSION verbatim. JS: `if (env.HERDR_BROWSER_SESSION) return` — truthy check → empty string falls through. bash: `[ -n "${...:-}" ]` — empty falls through. Match.
  
  Config file empty-after-strip: bash checks `[ -n "$cfg" ]` then falls through; JS `if (name) return name` then falls through. Match.
  
  WORKSPACE_ID: bash `[ -n ]`, JS truthy. Match.

**browse.sh**:
- `set -uo pipefail`. No lock around pane open — multiple browse panes allowed by design ("many-per-workspace").
- Appends pane id to browse-ids file. **Race**: two concurrent browse.sh invokes → `printf >> "$(browse_ids_file)"` — append is atomic-ish for small writes on POSIX (O_APPEND via shell redirection — yes, >> uses O_APPEND). Two concurrent appends of short lines won't interleave on local fs. OK.
- BUT: browse_ids_file content grows; close.sh reads ids and closes each; dead ids harmless. OK.
- **No validation that `--env HERDR_BROWSE_URL=$url` injection**... herdr handles args safely via array. OK.

**browse-pane.sh**:
- Prompts URL when HERDR_BROWSE_URL unset. `IFS= read -r url` — read from pane stdin. Empty → exit 0 → pane closes immediately! Comment in pane.sh says "an exiting pane process closes the pane before the user can read the error" — here exit 0 closes pane, but it's user's choice (hit enter with empty). Fine.
- URL prefix: `case "$url" in http://*|https://*) ;; *) url="https://$url" ;; esac` — note: **no flag validation** like validate_url. A url of `-foo` → becomes `https://-foo` → harmless. But a url like `--user-agent=evil` typed by user → `https://--user-agent=evil` → invalid URL → carbonyl errors. Since prefixing happens for non-http inputs, carbonyl flag injection is mitigated. BUT what if user types `http://x` exactly → passes through unprefixed; `http://*` glob... fine. What about `HTTP://x` uppercase? `case` is case-sensitive → falls to `*)` → `https://HTTP://x` → carbonyl navigates to host "http" path... weird but harmless.
- **zoom parsing**: `tr -cd '0-9'` — strips non-digits. zoom="1a2b" → "12". zoom file "abc" → empty → `${zoom:-100}` → 100. zoom "0" → `--zoom=0`?? carbonyl zoom 0 — degenerate but user's config. zoom "999999999999" → carbonyl clamps? Whatever. P2 nit. Also HERDR_BROWSE_ZOOM env is used RAW (not digit-stripped): `--zoom="${zoom:-100}"` with zoom from env — if env contains spaces/flags... `--zoom=--no-sandbox`? Since it's one quoted arg `--zoom=...`, carbonyl sees a single arg `--zoom=<garbage>` → invalid value error. Not flag injection (the = form binds it). OK.
- **exec carbonyl --zoom="$zoom" "$url"** — url quoted ✓.

**close.sh**:
- Closes tracked panes, then strays via label match, then `agent-browser --session close`.
- **`for stray in $strays`** — unquoted word splitting: pane ids with spaces would break; pane ids don't contain spaces (per lib.sh comment). OK.
- `while IFS= read -r id; do close_pane "$id"; done < "$f"` — reads pane-id file. Note pane_id_file holds ONE id (open.sh overwrites with `>`), browse_ids file holds many.
- **BUG?**: `pane_alive` pre-check skipped deliberately (comment). OK.
- `rm -f "$sd/shot-$ws.png"` — matches renderer's shot path naming: `shot-${HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID || 'default'}.png` in stateDir. bash: `$sd/shot-$ws.png` where sd = state_dir() = HERDR_PLUGIN_STATE_DIR or ~/.local/state/herdr-browser; JS: same env or ~/.local/state/herdr-browser. Match ✓.
- **The stray `~/` directory in the repo**: `/Users/vics/dev/structupath/herdr-browser/~/.local/state/herdr/plugins/structupath.browser/pane-id-w2`. Interesting — path contains `herdr/plugins/structupath.browser` NOT `herdr-browser`. So some OTHER code (herdr itself, or an older version of this plugin with state dir `~/.local/state/herdr/plugins/structupath.browser`) created a literal `~` dir under the repo cwd. Which script? None of the current scripts reference `herdr/plugins`. Possibly herdr itself passing HERDR_PLUGIN_STATE_DIR="~/.local/..." unexpanded and an old script did `mkdir -p "$HERDR_PLUGIN_STATE_DIR"` — tilde inside the env value never expands (tilde expansion only happens for literal tildes at start of word in bash, not from variable values!). **THAT'S THE BUG**: if herdr passes HERDR_PLUGIN_STATE_DIR as a literal `~/.local/state/...` string, `state_dir()`'s `mkdir -p "$d"` creates a literal `~` directory under the cwd. Evidence: the artifact exists in this repo! And the renderer JS: `fs.mkdirSync(this.stateDir, {recursive:true})` — same: literal `~` dir under process.cwd(). So the whole plugin can end up writing state into `<cwd>/~/...` instead of the home directory. Consequences: state_dir, pane-id files, shot PNGs all go to a per-cwd literal-tilde dir; close.sh computes the same (consistent within bash since all cd to plugin root...). Actually wait — pane.sh cds to plugin root; renderer runs with cwd=plugin root → literal ~ dir in plugin root = THIS REPO. That's exactly what we see: `/Users/vics/dev/structupath/herdr-browser/~/.local/state/herdr/plugins/structupath.browser/pane-id-w2`. So this HAPPENED. The mismatch: herdr plugins/structupath.browser path suggests herdr itself set HERDR_PLUGIN_STATE_DIR=~/.local/state/herdr/plugins/structupath.browser (with literal tilde, unexpanded) at some point, and state_dir() blindly mkdir'd it. Current code uses it verbatim too. So: **state_dir() and the Renderer both fail to expand a leading `~` in HERDR_PLUGIN_STATE_DIR** — if herdr passes tilde paths, state leaks into a literal `./~` tree. Evidence on disk proves it occurred. P1 (real, happened, may still happen depending on herdr version). The fix: expand leading ~/ in state_dir() and renderer constructor, or herdr-side. Also that stale dir should be deleted (not my job—report only).
  
  Hmm wait, but also: close.sh's rm of shots uses state_dir() — consistent. pane_id_file — consistent. So everything consistently wrong → works "by accident" but pollutes repo and breaks cross-invocation state if cwd differs (open.sh always cds to plugin root, so consistent). The panes work but state is in the repo. Fine — P1 correctness/hygiene finding with on-disk evidence.

- close.sh: `for f in "$(pane_id_file)" "$(browse_ids_file)"` — state_dir() called repeatedly; each call mkdirs. Fine.
- **session close even when other panes/workspaces share the session?** Session is per-workspace derived → fine.
- close.sh never fails silently — echoes summary ✓.
